answer: A do-nothing handler is included in the logging package: NullHandler (since Python 3.1). An instance of this handler could be added to the top-level logger of the logging namespace used by the library if you want to prevent your library’s logged events being output to sys.stderr in the absence of logging configuration. If all logging by a library is done using loggers with names matching the library, then adding NullHandler ensures that messages are not printed when no logging configuration is present.
